summary: High‑energy 4/12/69 Utah show shines on Dark Star, The Eleven and Lovelight; an analog SBD captures the vibe with pleasant hiss and occasional splice.
review: The performance is praised for its fiery Dark Star, scorching The Eleven, a hot Lovelight and solid Dew and He Was a Friend of Mine, despite the loss of most of The Other One. The soundboard source delivers a warm analog character – a little hiss, some pitch‑correction quirks and a splice in Lovelight – but remains clear enough to let the music breathe.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show highly (4‑5 stars) and highlight standout performances; recording quality is good with only minor analog noise and splice issues, yielding a strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Analog SBD with characteristic hiss and occasional pops; noticeable splice in Lovelight and slight pitch‑drift that some listeners correct. Minor cuts (missing parts of Dark Star/Other One) affect completeness but overall clarity is solid.

standout_songs: He Was a Friend of Mine
show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set university ballroom show with curfew‑induced truncation; early set opens with Dew and features the Yellow Dog joke, while the late set delivers Dark Star (with a brief missing intro) and a smoking Eleven. Despite cuts, the flow feels loose, experimental and exciting.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Noted for his agitated energy and strong guitar tone; interacts playfully with Pigpen.
band_member_comments.Bob: His Yellow Dog storytelling is highlighted as odd but entertaining; overall vocal presence praised.
